preamble.summary: The vision of the four beasts and the Son of Man. This is the most theologically significant chapter in Daniel. The OG and Theodotion are relatively close to the MT here, with both preserving the Son of Man figure in 7:13. Key variants concern the prepositions used with the Son of Man's approach to the Ancient of Days.
preamble.notable_variants: In 7:13, the OG reads 'as a son of man came, and he was present as the Ancient of Days' (hōs palaios hēmerōn parēn) — a striking variant suggesting the Son of Man IS the Ancient of Days. Theodotion reads 'came to the Ancient of Days and was presented before him' — matching the MT. This variant has enormous Christological implications.
preamble.structural_notes: All three versions have 28 verses. The OG's 7:13 is the most theologically significant variant in the entire book.

variant_notes: No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.

verses.variant_rendering: a fourth beast, fearful and astonishing
verses.mt_rendering: a fourth beast, dreadful and terrifying
variant_notes: Both Greek versions preserve the exceptional description. The OG adds that it was 'exceedingly strong' (ischyron perissos) — an intensification.
Both versions describe the ten horns and the little horn with eyes and a mouth. OG and Theodotion agree closely here.
Both Greek versions render Attiq Yomin as palaios hēmerōn. The description of the throne, fire, and garment is consistent across all versions.
Both versions preserve the cosmic court scene with its innumerable attendants.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.

verses.mt_reading: עִם־עֲנָנֵי שְׁמַיָּא כְּבַר אֱנָשׁ אָתֵה הֲוָה וְעַד־עַתִּיק יוֹמַיָּא מְטָה
verses.lxx_reading: OG: ὡς υἱὸς ἀνθρώπου ἤρχετο καὶ ὡς παλαιὸς ἡμερῶν παρῆν / Th: ὡς υἱὸς ἀνθρώπου ἐρχόμενος... ἕως τοῦ παλαιοῦ τῶν ἡμερῶν ἔφθασεν
verses.variant_rendering: OG: 'as a son of man was coming, and as the Ancient of Days he was present' / Th: 'as a son of man coming... he reached the Ancient of Days'
verses.mt_rendering: one like a son of man came with the clouds of heaven, and he reached the Ancient of Days
variant_notes: CRITICAL THEOLOGICAL VARIANT. The MT and Theodotion describe two figures: the Son of Man approaches the Ancient of Days and is presented before him. The OG appears to IDENTIFY the Son of Man with the Ancient of Days — 'as the Ancient of Days he was present' (hōs palaios hēmerōn parēn). This OG reading supports a divine Christology where the Son of Man shares the identity of the Ancient of Days. Early church fathers (including those who used the OG) cited this reading. The textual situation is complex and debated, but the OG variant is one of the most theologically consequential in all of Daniel.

verses.mt_rendering: dominion and glory and a kingdom
variant_notes: Both Greek versions preserve the threefold gift to the Son of Man. The universality of his dominion is emphasized in all traditions.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.

verses.variant_rendering: the holy ones of the Most High
verses.mt_rendering: the saints of the Most High
variant_notes: Both Greek versions render qaddishei elyonin as 'the holy ones of the Most High.' The identification of these figures — angels, Israel, or both — is debated.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.

verses.variant_rendering: made war with the holy ones
variant_notes: Both versions describe the horn's persecution. The language is echoed in Revelation 13:7.

verses.lxx_reading: ἕως τοῦ ἐλθεῖν τὸν παλαιὸν τῶν ἡμερῶν
verses.variant_rendering: until the Ancient of Days came
verses.mt_rendering: until the Ancient of Days came
variant_notes: OG adds that judgment was given 'against' (kata) the little horn, slightly different from MT/Theodotion which say judgment was given 'to/for' the saints.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.

verses.lxx_reading: καιρὸν καὶ καιροὺς καὶ ἥμισυ καιροῦ
verses.variant_rendering: a time, times, and half a time
verses.mt_rendering: a time, times, and half a time
variant_notes: All three versions agree on this enigmatic temporal formula (3.5 years). The OG and Theodotion translate identically. This formula recurs in Revelation 12:14.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
